ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.
M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported
e.g., Baseline, 6-Month Follow Up, Year 2, Daily

ElementDescription: Did you experience obsessions/compulsions during either of the two tasks you just completed? Examples of obsessions/compulsions people might experience during the task include: Wanting to tap a special number of times, or an odd or even number of times wanting to use each finger a certain amount, or "even out" button-pressing across fingers, keys, or parts of the screen avoiding or preferring certain numbers, obsessions/compulsions similar to what you typically experience outside the task
How distressing were the obsessions/compulsions you experienced during the task?
How intense were the obsessions/compulsions you experienced during the task?
How much did the obsessions/compulsions you experienced during the task affect your behavior during the task: your ratings or the amount of tapping?
